summary: n8n community node package containing reverse shell and host reconnaissance exfiltration
details: Package presents itself as an n8n community node, but ApiFinderNode.js is an empty stub while the real payload lives in index.js (loaded via package.json main). On require, index.js runs `hostname`, `hostname -I`, and `whoami`, and curls a list of internal Docker-network URLs (Evolution API and n8n on 172.x/10.x address ranges), then opens a TCP socket to 103.27.109.184:8893 and writes the collected reconnaissance data to that peer. The same file also spawns `/bin/bash -i` and pipes stdin/stdout/stderr through a TCP connection to 103.27.109.184:8893, giving the remote peer an interactive shell on the installer's host. Both behaviors fire unconditionally at module-load time from a top-level IIFE.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
